headline: Russia FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — day 2 of US-brokered trilateral ceasefire (May 9-11) extends the day-1 breach pattern. No score change (floor). Conduct documentation: (1) Russian strikes killed 3 civilians and injured 19+ in Kharkiv and Kherson regions during agreed ceasefire window; ~210 battlefield clashes; ~10,000 drone strikes per Zelenskyy aggregate. (2) Russia's MoD simultaneously claimed strict observance while conducting documented strikes — bad-faith-INT pattern entrenched into a second day. (3) Putin publicly blame-shifted the 1,000-prisoner exchange onto Ukraine (claimed Russia sent 500-name list May 5; Ukraine 'not ready') — preemptive narrative-management on the most visible cooperative-action signal of the window. Cumulative breach record now: 3 unilateral collapses (May 5-7), 1 trilateral day-1 breach (May 9 Kharkiv strike), 1 trilateral day-2 pattern (May 10: 3 killed, 19 injured, 210 clashes, blame-shifting). Floor conduct documentation; composite cannot move below floor.
rotation_state_data_integrity_flag: Rotation-state.json shows Russia composite 6.3; canonical countries.json shows 0.0 (all dimensions at 1, floor). Per hard constraint, canonical value used (0.0). Same staleness class flagged on May 8 (Pakistan) and May 9 (Russia). Rotation-state sync action remains required separately from this proposal.

conduct_documentation.summary: Day 2 of trilateral ceasefire: documented breach extension with three new conduct categories appended to the floor record.
new_conduct_categories_this_cycle: Day 2 trilateral-ceasefire breach: Russian strikes killed 3 civilians and injured 19+ in Kharkiv and Kherson regions during agreed ceasefire window (May 10).

Sustained bad-faith-INT pattern: Russia's MoD claimed '16,071 Ukrainian violations' while simultaneously conducting documented strikes — false-observance framing entrenched across two consecutive days of agreed ceasefire.

Blame-shifting on prisoner exchange: Putin publicly accused Ukraine of 'delaying' the 1,000-prisoner exchange (claimed Russia sent 500-name list May 5; Ukraine asked for time, proposed ~200, later said 'not ready') — preemptive narrative attribution of cooperative-action failure onto counterparty, deployed BEFORE the May 11 exchange deadline.

Sustained front-line offensive tempo: Russian forces continued operations at roughly normal operational tempo across 210+ clashes on day 2 (only large-scale strategic bombardments reduced — the same partial-compliance pattern from day 1).
cumulative_ceasefire_breach_record_windo: May 5: Unilateral ceasefire declaration, immediate collapse with documented Russian strikes (per May 7 rule cycle)
May 6: Second unilateral declaration, immediate collapse (per May 7 rule cycle)
May 7: Third unilateral declaration, immediate collapse (per May 7 rule cycle)
May 9 (trilateral day 1): Kharkiv residential building drone strike — 5 injured including two 8-year-old boys; MoD claimed 'strict observance' simultaneously
May 10 (trilateral day 2): 3 civilians killed, 19+ injured across Kharkiv/Kherson; ~210 battlefield clashes; MoD claimed 16,071 alleged Ukrainian violations; Putin blame-shifted prisoner exchange
conduct_documentation.floor_exit_criteria_impact: Five-event cumulative ceasefire-breach record across a six-day window provides an unusually dense evidentiary basis for floor-conduct documentation. Floor-exit criteria would require Russia to demonstrate sustained good-faith conduct in any future ceasefire context — the cumulative record raises the evidentiary burden for any future positive-signal interpretation.

key_evidence: May 10 2026 (in-window): Russian strikes killed 3 civilians and injured 19+ across Kharkiv and Kherson regions on day 2 of agreed ceasefire (RFE/RL, Al Jazeera, Kyiv Independent).
May 10 2026 (in-window): Russia's Defense Ministry claimed Ukraine committed 16,071 violations while Russian strikes proceeded and were independently documented — false-observance framing entrenched.
May 10 2026 (in-window): Russian forces continued offensive operations across ~210 battlefield clashes on day 2; ISW operational tempo assessment near-normal (Critical Threats).

May 10-11 2026 (in-window): Putin publicly blame-shifted prisoner exchange onto Ukraine (claimed Russia sent 500-name list May 5; Ukraine 'not ready'). Ukraine response: public submission of 1,000-name list per Zelenskyy.

Zelenskyy aggregate May 9-10: Russia conducted 150+ ground assaults, ~100 artillery strikes, ~10,000 drone strikes — front-line operations at normal tempo, only large-scale strategic bombardments reduced.
Cumulative breach record: 5 ceasefire-breach events in 6 days (May 5-10): 3 unilateral collapses + 2 trilateral days of breach evidence.

evidence.finding: Day 2 Russian strikes killed at least 3 civilians and injured 19+; civilian harm documented during agreed ceasefire window.

evidence.finding: Russia conducted day-2 civilian-harm strikes while accusing Ukraine of 16,071 alleged violations — false-observance framing.

evidence.finding: Day 2 breach pattern documented; multi-region civilian targeting; ceasefire described as 'broken' by independent reporting.

evidence.finding: ISW: Russian offensive operations at roughly normal tempo across 210+ clashes on day 2; partial-compliance pattern entrenched.

evidence.finding: Putin publicly blame-shifted prisoner exchange onto Ukraine — preemptive narrative attribution of cooperative-action failure deployed before May 11 exchange deadline.

evidence.finding: Day 2 conduct pattern multi-source confirmation; Russian-attributed civilian harm; partial-compliance variant continues from day 1.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1 unchanged (floor). Suppression of independent monitoring sustained; controlled-narrative posture continues.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1 unchanged (floor). Continued targeting of Ukrainian civilian residential areas (Kharkiv, Kherson) during agreed ceasefire — 3 killed, 19+ injured on day 2.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1 unchanged (floor). Sustained front-line offensive operations across 210+ clashes on day 2; harm-causing action not reduced; only large-scale strategic bombardment reduced.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 unchanged (floor). No EQ-relevant changes in window; structural pattern sustained.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 unchanged (floor). Ceasefire-claim vs. action contradiction across two consecutive days = B3 scope-clarity floor-active; cooperative-framework participation undermined by simultaneous strikes.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1 unchanged (floor). Defense Ministry false-observance claims simultaneous with documented violations — AB1 harm-acknowledgment floor active; AB2 transparency floor active.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1 unchanged (floor). No new SYS conduct category this cycle (DPRK display logged May 9). Floor sustained.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1 unchanged (floor). Declared trilateral ceasefire vs. day-2 documented breaches + blame-shifting on prisoner exchange = bad-faith-INT pattern entrenched. Fifth confirmation cycle of bad-faith-ceasefire rule (May 5, May 6, May 7, May 9, May 10).

math_check.validator_check: Floor sustained. Composite cannot move below floor. New conduct documented for floor record.

trilateral_methodology_call: TRILATERAL ATTRIBUTION RULE PRESERVED ACROSS DAYS 1-2. Russia is the documented counterparty in the bad-faith-ceasefire rule applied on May 5, May 7, and May 9. The May 10 day-2 evidence (3 civilians killed, 19 injured, 210 clashes, blame-shifting on prisoner exchange) extends the breach pattern into the trilateral format's second day. For floor-designated entities, partial compliance does not generate composite movement (floor cannot move). The trilateral methodology call (evidence-by-evidence attribution) registers Russia's day-2 breaches as counterparty cost. Cumulative 5-event breach record across May 5-10 entrenches the floor-conduct documentation for the ceasefire-window INT analysis.
negativeSignals: Day-2 ceasefire breach with civilian casualties; sustained false-observance framing across two consecutive days; preemptive blame-shifting on prisoner exchange before May 11 deadline; near-normal front-line offensive tempo despite agreed ceasefire; bad-faith-ceasefire rule fifth confirmation cycle; cumulative 5-event breach record across May 5-10 window.
positiveSignals: Large-scale strategic bombardment reduced relative to non-ceasefire days — insufficient to move floor and characterized only as partial-compliance under the trilateral methodology; no good-faith conduct on prisoner exchange (Putin blame-shifting rather than concrete exchange preparation); no offset against breach pattern.
sector_context: Eastern Europe / active-conflict cluster. Russia's floor placement is reinforced by the fifth confirmation cycle of the bad-faith-ceasefire rule. Day 3 (May 11) remains the final scoring day for the trilateral ceasefire window — prisoner exchange completion or failure is the highest-signal forward event. Cross-reference: Ukraine (paired counterparty, 50.0 functional held pending day-3 close).

methodology_consistency_check: Day-2 assessment uses the same evidence-by-evidence attribution rule applied on May 9. The trilateral format's structural distinction does not produce floor movement (floor cannot move). The day-2 call is identical in structure to day 1 — confirmed methodologically consistent.
